archetypes.name: CLI Command
archetypes.description: CLI command handlers and subcommand implementations.

analysisHints.description: CLI command modules handle user-facing terminal commands

archetypes.name: Component
archetypes.description: React/UI component files with JSX-returning functions.

analysisHints.description: Components have inflated call counts from hooks, state setters, and JSX rendering
High fan-in expected — config modules are widely imported

archetypes.name: Entry Point
archetypes.description: Module entry points, public APIs, and CLI entry files where uncalled exports are expected.

analysisHints.description: Uncalled exports are expected in entry points

archetypes.name: Gateway
archetypes.description: Re-export-heavy gateway modules that concentrate cross-package imports.

analysisHints.description: Gateway modules are expected to re-export heavily

archetypes.name: Hook
archetypes.description: React hooks — custom hooks encapsulating reusable stateful logic.

analysisHints.description: React hooks follow the use* naming convention

archetypes.name: Model
archetypes.description: Data models, schemas, and ORM definitions.

archetypes.name: Page
archetypes.description: Page-level components, views, or screens — top-level UI entry points for routes.

analysisHints.description: Page/view components orchestrate many sub-components and have inflated call counts

archetypes.name: Route Handler
archetypes.description: Server-side HTTP route handlers (Express, Hono, Koa, etc.).

archetypes.name: Route Module
archetypes.description: Framework convention route modules (Remix/React Router) with loader/action/default exports.

analysisHints.description: Convention exports are expected in route modules

archetypes.name: Schema
archetypes.description: Runtime validation schemas and data shape definitions (Zod, Yup, Joi, etc.).

analysisHints.description: Schema modules define runtime data shapes for validation

archetypes.name: Service
archetypes.description: Service layer modules — API clients, data fetching, and business logic orchestration.

analysisHints.description: Service modules orchestrate business logic and external calls

archetypes.name: Store
archetypes.description: State management stores and slices where high fan-in is expected.

analysisHints.description: High fan-in is expected for state management modules

archetypes.name: Types & Constants
archetypes.description: Type definitions, constants, and enums — companion files, not logic.

analysisHints.description: Unidirectional coupling and high fan-in expected for type files

archetypes.name: Utility
archetypes.description: Shared utility, helper, and infrastructure modules where high fan-in is expected.

analysisHints.description: High fan-in is expected for utility modules

evidence.detail: Top-level CI pipeline script that orchestrates subprocesses; not a CLI command handler but a standalone infrastructure/build utility
Integration module for Claude API — API client / business logic orchestration.
Top-level help output handler for the CLI.
Adaptive analysis logic — business logic for adjusting agent behavior.
Review analysis — business logic that evaluates agent output.
Spin/loop detection analysis — business logic service.
Stuck-state detection analysis — business logic service.
Summary generation analysis — business logic service.
Workflow analysis logic — business logic service.
Agent completion handling — core business logic for determining run completion.
CLI-mode agent loop — orchestrates the agent execution lifecycle for CLI context.
Heartbeat management — service that tracks agent liveness during execution.
Implements the main agent turn loop — orchestrates API calls, tool dispatch, token tracking, and run lifecycle. It is business logic orchestration with no HTTP routing, no UI, and no data modeling.
Shared lifecycle utilities — helper functions shared across lifecycle modules.
Task display formatting — utility for rendering task state to the terminal.
Token budget management — service logic for enforcing token limits.
Token usage tracking — utility helpers for accumulating and reporting usage.
Brief generation — service that constructs the task brief for the agent.
Prompt construction — utility helpers for assembling LLM prompts.
Agent tool definitions and dispatch — shared utility configuring available tools.
CLI help command implementation for the hench package.
Guard command handling — service logic for command safety checks.
Guard contract type definitions and constants — no logic, only shapes and enums.
Path utilities for the guard system — helper functions for path validation.
Guard policy evaluation — business logic determining what commands are allowed.
Explicitly documented as a centralized gateway concentrating all hench→rex cross-package runtime imports as re-exports
Concurrent execution metrics — utility for tracking parallelism and performance data.
Shell execution helper — low-level utility wrapping shell command invocation.
Process execution utility — shared helper for spawning and managing child processes.
Manages process lifecycle — start/stop/restart logic for agent processes
Rate/concurrency limiting utility for process management
Monitors memory usage as a background service concern
Throttling utility based on memory pressure
Process pool management — orchestrates worker processes
Tracks per-process memory consumption over time
Task execution queue — coordinates work item ordering and dispatch
Priority-based scheduling logic for the execution queue
Shared glob/file-matching utility used across hench modules
Type definitions and interfaces for tool contracts — no runtime logic
Dispatches tool calls to the appropriate tool handler
Low-level shell execution utility wrapping child_process
File operation utilities used by the agent tool layer
Git command utilities for the agent tool layer
Rex integration tool — bridges hench agent to rex PRD operations
Shell invocation helpers and abstraction over exec-shell
Orchestrates test execution as part of agent tool use
Validates task completion criteria after agent runs
API-based LLM provider implementation — handles HTTP calls to LLM APIs
Authentication helpers (API key resolution, credential management)
CLI-subprocess-based LLM provider — spawns Claude CLI for completions
Factory that instantiates the correct LLM client based on config
Low-level subprocess execution utility for CLI providers
Formats help/error messages for LLM client output
JSON parsing/extraction utilities for LLM response handling
Core LLM client abstraction — main interface for sending prompts and receiving completions
Shared type definitions for the LLM client package
Config loading and deep-merge helper functions shared across packages — infrastructure utility, not a config file itself.
Directory path resolution helpers — shared infrastructure utility.
Pure TypeScript interfaces and type definitions (LLMProvider, ProviderInfo, StreamChunk, etc.) — no runtime logic.
ProviderRegistry class with factory registration, vendor selection, and singleton defaultRegistry — service layer for provider lifecycle management.
Session management for LLM providers — stateful service logic.
Pure parsing utility functions (parseApiTokenUsage, parseCliTokenUsage, parseStreamTokenUsage) — no state or business logic.
Business logic for acknowledging/suppressing sourcevision findings — domain service.
Shared constants (DEFAULT_MODEL) and types (ClaudeResult) for the analyze module — companion types/constants file.
Guard logic preventing invalid consolidation operations — validation utility.
Decomposes PRD proposals into structured items — domain service.
Deduplication logic for PRD items — pure utility.
Computes diffs between PRD states — pure utility.
Extraction business logic for pulling structured data from LLM output — domain service.
File existence and format validation — utility.
Interactive guided spec-builder workflow — domain service orchestrating user prompts and LLM calls.
Module-level LLM client singleton, config management, and spawnClaude dispatch — central LLM interaction service.
LLM-based reason generation for PRD modifications — domain service.
Generates PRD proposals from analysis — core domain service.
LLM reasoning orchestration for PRD analysis — domain service.
Reconciles proposed changes against existing PRD state — domain service.
LLM-based reasoning for PRD reshape/reorganize proposals — domain service.
Codebase scanning functions that collect project signals for PRD generation — domain service.
Validates proposed PRD modifications before applying — validation utility.
CLI help content definitions and formatHelp wrappers — utility module for rendering help text.
MCP tool handler implementations (handleGetPrdStatus, handleAddItem, etc.) — service layer decoupled from transport.
Creates and starts the Rex MCP server for the 'rex mcp' CLI command — CLI entry point for MCP transport.
Detects conflicts between recommendations and existing PRD items — validation/detection utility.
Creates PRD items from sourcevision recommendations — domain service orchestrating store writes.
Single constant string export (DEFAULT_WORKFLOW template), no logic
Defines BUILTIN_ARCHETYPES constant array — the configuration data that drives the classification engine
Pure functions for classifying change significance and breaking change status — no I/O, no side effects
Orchestrates collection of completed PRD items from filesystem and git history — I/O-heavy service layer
Pure filtering functions for branch-scoped work items plus git lifecycle detection helpers
Persistent JSON file storage for branch work records — handles save/load with Zod validation
Generates deterministic architectural findings from call graph data — analysis service with configurable thresholds
TypeScript AST-based call graph analyzer — primary analysis engine that reads files and builds the graph
File classification engine orchestrating algorithmic and LLM-based archetype assignment
LLM API client bridge — vendor-aware client with module-level state for Claude/Codex access
Reads and processes PRD completion status from filesystem — data extraction service with aggregation logic
React component and route analyzer — AST-based extraction engine with I/O and multiple analysis phases
Generates CONTEXT.md from analysis data — output generation service orchestrating multiple data sources
Batch LLM enrichment with retry logic and prompt construction — orchestrates zone enrichment calls
Configuration constants (ZONES_PER_BATCH, PASS_CONFIGS, timeouts) and prompt builders for enrichment passes
Pure JSON parsing, finding extraction, zone merging, and deduplication helpers — no I/O
Per-zone AI enrichment orchestrator — manages concurrent zone enrichment with incremental skip logic
Explicit barrel re-export hub consolidating enrich-config, claude-client, enrich-parsing, token-usage, and enrich-per-zone into one stable import surface
TypeScript AST-based import graph analyzer — reads files, resolves module specifiers, builds the full import graph
File inventory analyzer — walks the directory tree, hashes files, classifies roles, supports incremental mode
Generates structured llms.txt Markdown from analysis data — output generation service
Pure Louvain community detection algorithm implementation — no I/O, all exported functions are deterministic graph algorithms
Reads and writes the sourcevision manifest.json with git metadata — file I/O service
Pure derivation of prioritized next steps from analysis findings — no I/O, all logic is functional
Pure computation module — calculates architectural risk scores and emits findings from cohesion/coupling metrics. No IO, no commands, shared across analyzers.
Detects server-side API routes via AST parsing and comment scanning — reads files and produces route groups
Pure token usage accumulation and formatting utilities — no I/O, shared helper functions
Workspace aggregation orchestrator — merges member inventories, imports, and zones; writes unified output files
Computes cross-repo zone crossings from external package imports — reads package.json files and resolves sibling member dependencies
Detects sub-analyses and promotes their zones/crossings into the parent — filesystem scanning and zone transformation service
Computes deterministic hashes for zones — pure utility with no side effects
Orchestrates writing per-zone output files (context.md, summary.json) — side-effectful service logic
Core analysis service implementing Louvain community detection, zone metric computation, and structural insight generation — business logic orchestration, not a simple utility.
Likely contains static help text strings/constants for CLI display — no logic
CLI command handler for the sourcevision mcp subcommand
CLI command handler for the sourcevision serve subcommand
PDF export logic — data transformation and file generation service
Template/string generation utility for PR markdown output
Shared merge helper — pure utility module
Canonical JSON sorting utilities — pure, high fan-in utility
Vanilla JS module implementing landing page interactions (theme toggle, animations, terminal demo) — business logic orchestration with no framework
Caching layer for aggregated data — stateful service with business logic
Tracks concurrent execution metrics — stateful monitoring service
Explicitly named gateway — re-exports sourcevision cross-package imports
Tracks incremental task token usage — stateful service
MCP dependency wiring/shared deps — infrastructure utility module
Diagnostics collection for PR markdown refresh — monitoring service
Tracks process memory over time — stateful monitoring service
Explicitly named gateway — re-exports rex cross-package imports
Builds and queries a search index — stateful data service
Server entry point that wires HTTP server, all routes, WebSocket, watchers, and graceful shutdown — the top-level composition root for the web server.
Explicitly described as a 'public facade' that re-exports two internal modules (incremental-task-usage and usage-cleanup-scheduler) with no logic of its own.
Schedules periodic cleanup of usage data — background service
Standalone WebSocket protocol implementation (RFC 6455) providing connection management, broadcasting, health tracking, and keepalive — a reusable infrastructure utility.
Shared constants for data file paths — no logic, companion definitions
Filters/culls graph nodes for display — pure transformation utility
Shared deduplication helper for HTTP requests — infrastructure utility with high fan-in potential.
Bootstrap file initializes and wires up the viewer — classic entry point pattern.
Graph physics calculations — pure algorithmic helper for the renderer.
Graph rendering logic — imperative canvas/WebGL rendering helper, not a React component.
Data loading orchestration for the viewer — fetches and prepares data for display.
Rate-limiting helper for outbound calls — reusable infrastructure utility.
Fetch pipeline orchestrates HTTP messaging flow — service-layer concern.
Coalesces multiple messages into batches — reusable messaging utility.
Throttles message emission — reusable infrastructure utility.
WebSocket pipeline manages connection lifecycle and message flow — service concern.

Standalone module implementing crash detection and recovery logic — manages sessionStorage state, tracks crash history, and exposes a public API for detection and navigation state persistence. No framework dependencies, pure business logic orchestration.
Standalone zero-dependency DOM performance monitoring module — shared infrastructure consumed by a separate Preact hook
Gates DOM updates based on performance budget — infrastructure utility.
Manages feature degradation under performance pressure — infrastructure utility.
Standalone zero-dependency memory monitoring module — shared infrastructure consumed by a separate Preact hook
Gates response buffering based on performance state — infrastructure utility.
Batches DOM/state updates for efficiency — infrastructure utility.
Dispatches batched polling ticks — low-level polling infrastructure.
Orchestrates the polling lifecycle — service-layer coordination.
Handles polling restart logic — focused helper utility.
Holds polling state shared across the polling subsystem — state container with fan-in.
Wraps Page Visibility API — reusable browser utility.
Manages tick interval timing — low-level timer utility.
Gates tick emission based on tab visibility — infrastructure utility.
Schema compatibility/migration helpers — utility bridging schema versions.
General viewer utilities — shared helpers with high fan-in.
Color palette constants and mappings — pure constants/types, no logic.
Flow layout calculations for visualization — algorithmic helper utility.
Visualization metrics computations — pure calculation utility.

Business logic module that orchestrates a multi-step PR validation pipeline (build + rex validate), exporting a runPRCheck function with injected dependencies. Not a CLI entry point itself — it's imported and invoked by the orchestrator.
Shared utility exporting refreshSourcevisionDashboardArtifacts — a stateless helper that inspects and writes artifact metadata files. No domain logic, pure infrastructure helper.

Exports buildRefreshPlan which encapsulates business logic for translating CLI flags into a validated execution plan with steps, skipped steps, and notes. Domain orchestration logic, not a raw utility.

Exports snapshot, validation, and rollback functions (snapshotRefreshState, validateRefreshStep, validateRefreshCompletion, rollbackRefreshState) that together form a stateful validation/rollback service for the refresh pipeline.

Exports runWeb — a server lifecycle orchestration module handling start/stop/status subcommands, PID file management, background daemon mode, and port resolution. Service-layer orchestration, not a CLI handler itself.
